Cameron Boozer: NPOY frontrunner. Won ACC tourney title. 22.5 PPG + 10.2 RPG + 41% from 3. Best player in the field.
Isaiah Evans: Duke's #2 scorer. Benefits from doubled attention on Boozer. Consistent 14.9 PPG all season.
SEC tourney star: 30 pts + 11 ast in championship. 22.7 PPG + 6.2 APG leads SEC in both. Playing through ankle but clearly healthy enough — scored 37 in conf tourney.
AJ Dybantsa: Nation's leading scorer at 25.3 PPG. Projected #1 NBA pick. Dropped 43 vs Utah and 40 in Big 12 tourney. If BYU wins one game, he outscores most players ranked above him.
20+ pts in 4 straight games entering tourney. Leads all D-I in assists at 9.2 APG while scoring 15.7 PPG. Peaking at the perfect time.
Zuby Ejiofor: First unanimous Big East POY since Doug McDermott. Won Big East tourney MOP with 18 pts, 9 reb, 7 blocks in title game. Also Defensive POY and Scholar-Athlete of the Year.
Tyler Tanner: Breakout sophomore: 19.2 PPG, 5.3 APG, 2.4 SPG, 37% from 3. Vanderbilt has a sneaky-good draw at 5-seed.
Graham Ike: Most underappreciated player in college basketball. 19.7 PPG career-high this season. Has averaged 16+ PPG in four straight seasons. Gonzaga's anchor without Huff.
Braden Smith: Big Ten tourney MOP. Set conference tourney record with 46 assists. 1 assist from breaking Bobby Hurley's all-time NCAA career record. Will break it in R1 vs Queens.
John Blackwell: Wisconsin has 4 wins over top-10 KenPom teams scoring 90+ each time. Blackwell at 19.0 PPG is their engine. Team is peaking.
Mikey Lewis: Averaging 22.6 PPG over last 5 games. Dropped 31 on 7 threes vs #9 Gonzaga. Season avg of 14.2 PPG hides a massive late-season surge.
Tavari Johnson: MAC tourney MVP. 20.1 PPG scorer on a 12-seed. If Akron upsets Texas Tech (missing Toppin), his value doubles. Classic mid-major sleeper.
Brayden Burries: 21 pts in Big 12 championship win. Arizona's go-to scorer in big moments. Team has deepest expected games in the field.
Koa Peat: 21 pts alongside Burries in Big 12 title game. Arizona's balanced attack means multiple players produce.
Jaden Bradley: Big 12 POY. 13.3 PPG but the engine of Arizona's #1-seed offense. Steadiest hand in the tournament.
Kingston Flemings: Houston's star freshman. #5 in CBS NBA Draft rankings. Excellent shot-maker with March hero potential.
Keaton Wagler: Illinois star freshman. #6 in CBS NBA Draft rankings. Can go off for 25+ any given night.
Trey Kaufman-Renn: 20 pts in Big Ten championship. Purdue's inside presence. Benefits from Smith's historic assist rate.
Oscar Cluff: 21 pts in Big Ten tourney. Purdue's third scorer who elevates in big games. Season avg of 10.3 understates his tournament ceiling.
Fletcher Loyer: 14 pts + 5 ast in Big Ten final. Purdue's steady backcourt piece. Team won 4 games in 4 days to take Big Ten title.
Ryan Conwell: Louisville dark horse Final Four pick (NBC Sports). 18.7 PPG. Team ranks 19th in offensive efficiency.
Labaron Philon: Doubled his PPG from 10.6 as freshman to 21.7. Shooting 40% from 3, 46% on floaters, 66% at rim. Playing through injury but producing at elite level. Gets bigger role with Holloway OUT.
Aden Holloway: ARRESTED March 16 on felony marijuana charges. Was averaging 16.8 PPG. Status unknown — marked OUT. Alabama's KenPom adjusted down without him.
Bruce Thornton: Ohio St. 8-seed scorer at 20.2 PPG. 8/9 matchups are coin flips — if they advance, Thornton could put up 40+ over 2 games.
Bennett Stirtz: Iowa's 20.0 PPG scorer as a 9-seed. High-volume shooter who can erupt. Solid sleeper value.
Rueben Chinyelu: Florida's X-factor breakout big man. Only 11.2 PPG but on a 1-seed team with 4.32 expected games. Ceiling is high.
A-10 tourney MVP. Led VCU's 16-1 run. 14.4 PPG on a team that faces wounded UNC (missing Wilson). Huge upset potential.
TJ Power: Hit the game-tying 3 to force OT in Ivy League championship. Penn upset Yale 88-84. Clutch performer entering the tournament.
Thijs De Ridder: Virginia's top scorer at 15.5 PPG on a 3-seed with 2.76 expected games. Quietly one of the better value picks.
Duke Miles: Vanderbilt's second scorer at 16.4 PPG. Tanner + Miles give Vanderbilt a potent 1-2 punch on a 5-seed.
Boopie Miller: SMU's top scorer at 19.2 PPG. Low expected games (1.18) but if SMU survives First Four, his upside jumps.
Utah St. 9-seed scorer at 17.6 PPG. Faces Villanova in a competitive 8/9 matchup.
